# sim.admixture_demography_source_gene_flow { #tspaint.sim.admixture_demography_source_gene_flow }

```python
tspaint.sim.admixture_demography_source_gene_flow(
    Ne=1000,
    T_admix=30.0,
    census_offset=1.0,
    T_prev_admix=1000.0,
    T_split=5000.0,
    f_A=0.5,
    prev_migration=0.1,
)
```

Two-source admixture where the **sources themselves exchanged genes via a prior pulse**.

Like :func:`admixture_demography`, but with a mass migration ``SOURCE_A -> SOURCE_B`` (backward
in time; = a forward-time pulse of ``SOURCE_B`` **into** ``SOURCE_A``) of fraction
``prev_migration`` at ``T_prev_admix`` — placed between the census and the split. So the
``SOURCE_A`` reference panel genuinely carries ``~prev_migration`` of ``SOURCE_B`` introgressed
tracts: **real, localized reference contamination from a demographic event** (not a synthetic
pure/impure mix). The post-pulse census at ``T_admix + census_offset`` still labels each query
lineage's proximate source (A / B) — the prior pulse acts *deeper* than the census, so the query
local-ancestry truth is unchanged. (The A-refs' B tracts sit below the census, so they are not
in :func:`local_ancestry_truth`; the query A/B truth is the evaluation target.)

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**Ne**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`admixture_demography`.

<code>[**T_admix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`admixture_demography`.

<code>[**census_offset**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`admixture_demography`.

<code>[**T_split**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`admixture_demography`.

<code>[**f_A**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`admixture_demography`.

<code>[**T_prev_admix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Time of the prior A↔B pulse (strictly between the census and ``T_split``).

<code>[**prev_migration**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.1]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Fraction of ``SOURCE_A`` replaced (backward) by ``SOURCE_B`` at ``T_prev_admix`` — the ``SOURCE_A`` panel's foreign (B) fraction.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[msprime](`msprime`).[Demography](`msprime.Demography`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Demography with populations A, B, ADMIX and ANCESTRAL plus the admixture and census events, the prior ``SOURCE_A -> SOURCE_B`` mass-migration pulse at ``T_prev_admix``, and the A/B split at ``T_split``.

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

<code>[:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ValueError](`ValueError`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   If ``T_admix < census < T_prev_admix < T_split`` is violated or ``prev_migration`` ∉ ``[0,1)``.
